title: Continuous uniform distribution

mathlib_decls.evidence: decl --live: EXISTS in Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Uniform; def `IsUniform X s ℙ μ` states `map X ℙ = ProbabilityTheory.cond μ s` with `μ` defaulting to `volume` (Lebesgue), so for `s = [a,b] ⊂ ℝ` this is the continuous uniform U(a,b).
decl --live: EXISTS; the indicator-based PDF `s.indicator ((μ s)⁻¹ • 1)` — for `s = [a,b]` under Lebesgue this is `1/(b-a)` on the interval, the density of U(a,b).
decl --live: EXISTS; theorem identifying the PDF with the normalized indicator on s.
decl --live: EXISTS; ℙ(X⁻¹ A) = μ(s ∩ A)/μ(s), the defining property of uniform distribution.

qid_reasoning: `IsUniform` defaults its reference measure to `volume` (Lebesgue) and its canonical use case is uniform distribution on a real interval/region, i.e. the continuous uniform distribution Q274506; the broader Q59394126 (uniform distribution) would also cover discrete uniform, which Mathlib handles separately via `ProbabilityTheory.uniformOn`.
notes: `IsUniform` is the canonical formalization; `uniformPDF` gives the density and is the closest companion to the article's PDF formula.
